issues.title: Should we allow downgrade/fallback?
issues.author: DavidSchinazi
issues.body:
The original design in draft-schinazi-httpbis-ohttp-pfs-00 uses a BHTTP header to send the client's KEM public key `pkC` to the gateway. As @martinthomson points out, that's a layering violation that requires the OHTTP layer to know about the bits its encrypting instead of treating them as opaque.

We could put this into the OHTTP request format, but that would break backwards compatibility and remove the gateway's ability to choose to skip PFS in some cases.

If we went that route, we'd need something like the extensions from [draft-schinazi-httpbis-ohttp-ext-key-config](https://davidschinazi.github.io/draft-schinazi-httpbis-ohttp-ext-key-config/draft-schinazi-httpbis-ohttp-ext-key-config.html) to allow the gateway to tell the client that it supports PFS.

Both options are reasonable to me.
